questions.q: Explain the concept of banking services. What are the different types of banking services offered by commercial banks? (a) Current Account, (b) Savings Account, (c) Fixed Deposit Account. (d) Other services like Locker facility, (e) Mobile Banking. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Banking services refer to the various activities performed by commercial banks to facilitate financial transactions and provide financial assistance to customers. The different types of banking services offered by commercial banks include: (a) Current Account: A current account is a deposit account that allows customers to deposit and withdraw money as and when required. It is used for business purposes. (b) Savings Account: A savings account is a type of deposit account that earns interest on the deposited amount. It is used for personal purposes. (c) Fixed Deposit Account: A fixed deposit account is a type of deposit account where the customer deposits a fixed amount for a fixed period and earns interest on it. (d) Other services like Locker facility: Commercial banks offer locker facility to customers to store valuable items. (e) Mobile Banking: Mobile banking is an online banking service that allows customers to access their bank accounts and perform various transactions using their mobile phones. These services are offered by commercial banks to provide convenience and accessibility to customers.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of banking services (1 mark)
Mention 2 types of banking services offered by commercial banks (2 marks)
Describe the remaining 3 types of banking services (2 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the importance of insurance services in our economy. Explain the different types of insurance services offered by insurance companies. (a) Life Insurance, (b) General Insurance, (c) Health Insurance, (d) Motor Insurance, (e) Other types of insurance. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Insurance services play a vital role in our economy as they provide financial protection to individuals and businesses against various risks. The different types of insurance services offered by insurance companies include: (a) Life Insurance: Life insurance provides financial protection to the family of the insured in case of his or her untimely death. (b) General Insurance: General insurance covers risks related to property, liability, and accidents. (c) Health Insurance: Health insurance provides financial protection against medical expenses. (d) Motor Insurance: Motor insurance provides financial protection against losses or damages to vehicles. (e) Other types of insurance include Home Insurance, Travel Insurance, and Crop Insurance. These insurance services are essential for individuals and businesses to manage risks and protect their financial assets.
marking_scheme: Discuss the importance of insurance services (1 mark)
Mention 2 types of insurance services (2 marks)
Describe the remaining 3 types of insurance services (2 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of communication services. What are the different types of communication services offered by service providers? (a) Post Office Services, (b) Telephone Services, (c) Courier Services, (d) Telecommunication Services, (e) Internet Services. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Communication services refer to the various services provided by service providers to facilitate exchange of information between individuals, businesses, and organizations. The different types of communication services offered by service providers include: (a) Post Office Services: Post office services include sending and receiving letters, parcels, and money orders through the postal network. (b) Telephone Services: Telephone services include voice and video calls, SMS, and fax through mobile phones and landlines. (c) Courier Services: Courier services include delivery of packages, documents, and other items through specialized carriers like FedEx, UPS, and DHL. (d) Telecommunication Services: Telecommunication services include mobile phone services, internet services, and cable TV services. (e) Internet Services: Internet services include dial-up, broadband, and wireless internet services. These services are essential for individuals and businesses to communicate effectively and efficiently.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of communication services (1 mark)
Mention 2 types of communication services (2 marks)
Describe the remaining 3 types of communication services (2 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the importance of transport services in our economy. Explain the different types of transport services offered by transport providers. (a) Road Transport, (b) Rail Transport, (c) Air Transport, (d) Water Transport, (e) Other modes of transport. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Transport services play a vital role in our economy as they facilitate the movement of goods, services, and people from one place to another. The different types of transport services offered by transport providers include: (a) Road Transport: Road transport includes buses, trucks, and taxis that carry passengers and goods through roads. (b) Rail Transport: Rail transport includes trains that carry passengers and goods through rail networks. (c) Air Transport: Air transport includes airplanes that carry passengers and goods through air. (d) Water Transport: Water transport includes ships and boats that carry goods through waterways. (e) Other modes of transport include pipelines, cables, and satellite communication. These transport services are essential for the growth and development of our economy.
marking_scheme: Discuss the importance of transport services (1 mark)
Mention 2 types of transport services (2 marks)
Describe the remaining 3 types of transport services (2 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of warehousing services. What are the different types of warehousing services offered by warehouse providers? (a) Public Warehousing, (b) Private Warehousing, (c) Bonded Warehousing, (d) Cold Storage, (e) Other types of warehousing services. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Warehousing services refer to the storage and maintenance of goods in a warehouse until they are sold or transported to another location. The different types of warehousing services offered by warehouse providers include: (a) Public Warehousing: Public warehousing is a service provided by public warehouses where goods are stored for a short period of time. (b) Private Warehousing: Private warehousing is a service provided by private warehouses where goods are stored for a long period of time. (c) Bonded Warehousing: Bonded warehousing is a service provided by warehouses where goods are stored duty-free until they are sold or transported to another location. (d) Cold Storage: Cold storage is a service provided by warehouses where perishable goods are stored in a controlled environment. (e) Other types of warehousing services include automated warehousing, third-party logistics, and e-warehousing. These warehousing services are essential for businesses to manage their inventory and supply chain efficiently.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of warehousing services (1 mark)
Mention 2 types of warehousing services (2 marks)
Describe the remaining 3 types of warehousing services (2 marks)
